reasoning: C and A both deliver five genuinely distinct conceptual spaces, but C edges ahead by attacking a different deep given of the brief in each version: the salt garden turns the day into a solid object read by touch after the fact, the Shared Pulse relocates the day into a distributed temperature field across many people's water vessels (refusing the single-perceiver assumption), and the Tide Score makes a draining cistern tension a wire into a continuous rising pitch. A is nearly as strong and more polished, with the crowd-motion blob over a plaza and the content-dependent sand floor as standouts, but its five spaces overlap slightly less sharply on the assumption axis. B trails badly on diversity and craft: its texture wall, tasting menu, and thermal handrail are three restylings of one move (advance along a staged gradient), and the entries are thin one-line sketches rather than developed mechanisms.
